recommendation: Repair critical/high findings first; they are the strongest evidence that translated functionality has not converged.

prepContract.purpose: Preparation contract derived from phase-task-checklist and phase-capability-checklist. It summarizes what the current phase should implement by group and which groups still look risky or unresolved.

testHarnessStatus.latestFirstBlocker: ld.lld: error: cannot open Scrt1.o: No such file or directory
notes: The latest test build report is not PASS; test references are not strong evidence by themselves.
Current phase references tests; record a PASS, focused manual/emulator evidence, or a harness blocker.

implementationGroups.title: Verification scope: com/example
requiredBehaviors: ExampleInstrumentedTest is Android boilerplate that only asserts the application package name `com.example.foodike`; it is a verification asset that should be replaced rather than migrated verbatim.

ExampleUnitTest's `addition_isCorrect` method is a placeholder arithmetic assertion, so the target needs replacement verification for real mutation and persistent-state-update behavior instead of the literal source arithmetic.

ExampleUnitTest is boilerplate host-side test scaffolding with a trivial `addition_isCorrect` assertion, so it should be replaced with target-side verification of translated app behavior rather than copied verbatim.
riskHints: source tests should become target focused tests or explicit verification evidence
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests are not strong evidence until a latest test gate PASS or explicit harness blocker is recorded.
Source test intent must be represented by target tests, manual/emulator evidence, or a documented harness blocker.
architectureHints: If several source files are merged, keep business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints.

build pass alone does not close per-feature behavior
manual review evidence must point to concrete target endpoints

repairQueue.purpose: Full grouped repair queue for the translation agent. The queue indexes likely-related problems and evidence; each group still requires source/target readback, build/test checks, and authoritative matrix/capability updates before it can be considered repaired.
sweepGuidance: Process repair clusters in order and try to make progress on every listed cluster.
Within each cluster, sweep the referenced child groups instead of stopping after the first successful fix.
For each child group, verify source behavior and current target implementation before changing code or records.
When prep-contract groups are present, satisfy them as phase-level contracts before treating row-level findings as closed.
Implement real behavior or platform replacement when feasible; otherwise improve evidence or record a concrete blocker.
Do not stop after the first repaired group, and do not hard-close platform blockers without pure Cangjie evidence or a target-context probe.

clusters.title: Whole-project findings: implement-or-evidence
clusters.suggestedHandling: Treat this as the prompt-facing parent cluster. Sweep every child group listed in groupIds; use the child group items in translation-quality-summary.json for exact feature, capability, source path, endpoint, and evidence details.
clusters.blockerPolicy: If a child group is blocked by platform/product/tooling limits, record that blocker precisely and continue to the next child group instead of stopping the whole cluster.
